english: liberation, deliverance, so-só-tar-pai (so so thar pa'i) mdo (mdo) the book of deliverance, code of the moral law, containing about 250 precepts for the priesthood, the monastic rules of the Buddhists.
a medicinal herb, an emetic Wdn.

1. subst., also sógs-pa (sogs pa), shoulder-blade, scapula, sóg-pai (sog pa'i) mé-loṅ (me long) the flat part of it, sog-yu (sog yu) the narrow extremity of it; sog-mó (sog mo) 'débs-pa ('debs pa) (v. mo (mo) III.) to divine from the shoulder-blade; sog-lhú (sog lhu) shoulder as a piece of meat for boiling (I Sam. 9, 24). - 2. vb. (also: gsóg-pa (gsog pa), bsóg-pa (bsog pa), sógs-pa (sogs pa)) pf. (b)ags (bsags), fut. bsag (bsag), imp. sogs (sogs), bsag (bsag), W. *ság-če* (*sag ce*), to gather, heap up, hoard up, p'ral (phral) sog-ǰog-méd-pa (sog 'jog med pa) without having collected and deposited the daily requisites, the things wanted every day Mil., bsód-nams (bsod nams) sóg-pa (sog pa) Mil., tsogs (tshogs) sóg-pa (sog pa) frq. to collect, to hoard up merits of virtue, las-ṅán (las ngan) sóg-pa (sog pa) to heap up sins; gsog-ldáṅ (gsog ldang) morbid matter consisting in too great an accumulation of humours, ni f., Med.; dmág-gi (dmag gi) dpuṅ (dpung) sóg-pa (sog pa) to collect an army Dzl.; to assemble, children Glr.; hence *sag(s)* (*sag(s)*) W. all (of them), *lug (*lug) sag (sag) tsam* (tsam*) how many sheep are there in all?
a Mongol Glr., sóg-mo (sog mo) a Mongol woman, sog-prug (sog phrug) Mongol child, Mongol boy, sog-čás (sog chas) Mongol dress or fashion of dress, sog-rta (sog rta) Mongol horse.

blade, stalk; straw; sog-bru (sog bru) Sch. green corn that begins to sprout; sog-tsígs (sog tshigs) a knot on a stalk Cs.; sog-sbúr (sog sbur) a small blade of straw, chaff Pth.; sóg-mai (sog ma'i) bú-la (bu la) a shoe of straw; sog-rú (sog ru), sog-rúm (sog rum), sog-ldúm (sog ldum) stubbles.

B., C., *čad-sóg, (*cad sog,) gya-sóg* (gya sog*) W. a saw, sóg-les (sog les) gčód-pa (gcod pa) B. to saw to pieces, *gya-sóg (*gya sog) šrúl-ce* (shrul ce*) W. to saw; sog-le-k'a (sog le kha) the toothed edge of a saw, also botanical term,: serrate, serrated (of leaves) Wdn.

and so forth, and the like, mostly preceded by la (la): mí-la (mi la) sógs-pai (sogs pa'i) srog-čágs (srog chags) homo et cetera animantia, prop. the beings in addition to man; ba-dán-la (ba dan la) sógs-pas (sogs pas) brgyán-te (brgyan te) decorated with little flags and the like; less frq.: la (la) sógs-te (sogs te), inst. of which always la-sógs-pa (la sogs pa) or pai (pa'i) may be used; often sogs (sogs) alone, also in prose; after (la) (la) sógs(-pa) (sogs(-pa)) usually a comma is to be supplied, and the words following are to be considered as an apposition: yi-ge (yi ge) rtsís-la (rtsis la) sógs-pa (sogs pa) rig-pai (rig pa'i) gnas (gnas) lṅa (lnga) writing, arithmetic and so on, the five sciences; hence often applicable, when a comprehensive noun appellative does not exist: gser (gser) sogs (sogs) gold and the other, viz. metals, Glr.; tsa (tsa) sogs (sogs) gsum (gsum) the three tsa-sounds, tsa (tsa), tsha (tsha) and dza (dza) Gram. Note. In course of time the original grammatical sense seems to have been forgotten, in as much as la (la) is now read together with sógs-pa (sogs pa), and often also the dot separating the syllables is omitted.

perf. and imp. of gró-ba (gro ba) to go, 1. I went, I have (thou hast etc.) gone, v. gro-ba (gro ba) 1 and 2, e.g. der (der) soṅ (song) yód-pas (yod pas) when he had gone thither Pth., soṅ-sóṅ-ba-las (song song ba las) going on continually, Dzl., continuing to do a thing Dzl.; sóṅ-ba (song ba) yin (yin) it is gone, it is no longer extant Mil.; dbáṅ-du (dbang du) soṅ (song) (he or it) came into the power of... S.g.; da (da) sdíg-pa-la (sdig pa la) sóṅ-na (song na) Thgy. if we now go on to (the topic of sins) W.: *da-ruṅ (*da rung) ṅa (nga) ma (ma) soṅ* (song*) it is not yet past five o'clock; *'i-ne (*'i ne) sóṅ-pa (song pa) 'a ('a) tsúg-pa* (tsug pa*) from here (adverbially, like bzúṅs-te (bzungs te)) to that place Ld.; imp.: *dí-ru (*di ru) ma (ma) dug! (dug!) soṅ!* (song!*) do not stand here! walk on! — 2. became, turned etc. kyi-mo (khyi mo) źíg-tu (zhig tu) soṅ (song) she became a bitch, was changed into a bitch Mil., dkár-por (dkar por) soṅ (song) it turned white Glr., *doṅ (*dong) nág-po (nag po) soṅ* (song*) W. his face grew dark; *bi-gáṅ (*bi gang) soṅ* (song*) W. a hole has been made, it got a hole; *gyál-se (*gyal se) k'a-čúd (kha cud) sóṅ-ne* (song ne*) as she got a taste for the town W.; *nod (*nod) ču (chu) gaṅ (gang) soṅ* (song*) the vessel was already full of water (when I came) W. — *soṅ-tó* (*song to*) W. account of expenses.
1. C. to wake, rouse. — 2. sometimes for gsód-pa (gsod pa).
rarely for soṅ (song); frq. only in nár-son-pa (nar son pa), v. na (na).
1. v. son (son). — 2. v. gsón-pa (gson pa), gsón-po (gson po).
